name: Enyo
description: Enyo is a fearsome goddess of war in Greek mythology, often associated with the bloodlust and frenzy of battle. She is not a strategist or tactician like Athena, but rather embodies the raw, chaotic, and destructive aspects of warfare. Sometimes referred to as a sister or companion of Ares, the god of war, she revels in the carnage and bloodshed. Enyo is known for inciting madness and terror on the battlefield, driving soldiers to a state of unbridled fury. She is often depicted as a wild, unkempt woman, sometimes carrying weapons and accompanied by other war deities such as Deimos (Terror) and Phobos (Fear). Enyo is considered one of the Graeae (or Grey Sisters), daughters of Phorcys and Ceto, in some accounts, though her parentage varies across different sources. Her presence on the battlefield was said to inspire panic and facilitate the downfall of armies. She is less well-known than Ares, but her role in the chaotic reality of war is undeniable. Enyo thrives on the destruction and suffering caused by conflict, representing the unrestrained violence that can consume individuals and societies. Her influence is a chilling reminder of the darker side of human nature and the ever-present potential for war.
subtitle: Goddess of War's Bloodlust and Frenzy

keyMyths.title: Enyo and the Battlefield
keyMyths.description: Enyo is described as roaming battlefields, inciting terror and frenzy among the soldiers. Her presence signals imminent chaos and bloodshed, contributing to the overall devastation.

keyMyths.title: Association with Ares
keyMyths.description: Enyo is often found accompanying Ares in battle, serving as a close companion and embodying the destructive aspects of war. Their partnership amplifies the terror and violence of the conflict.

keyMyths.title: Enyo's Parentage
keyMyths.description: While accounts vary, Enyo is sometimes identified as one of the Graeae, daughters of Phorcys and Ceto. This connection links her to primordial sea deities and reinforces her ancient origins.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: A foundational text for Greek mythology, detailing the origins of the gods.
sources.text: A foundational text for Greek mythology, detailing the origins of the gods.
